meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Students: Build Your First App
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing to overcome the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier. Learn to implement pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert tutorial.
content.answer_block: Pricing isn't just a number; it's a product feature. To overcome 'no SaaS knowledge,' implement a tiered model: Free for acquisition, Pro for features, and Enterprise for support. By utilizing Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you transform abstract business logic into a functional, revenue-ready billing engine.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new SaaS project is intimidating, but the real paralysis sets in when you realize your code doesn't have a heartbeat—a revenue model. Most students treat pricing as an afterthought, burying it in the back-end, effectively killing their project's potential before a single user ever signs up. You aren't just writing code; you are building an engine for value exchange.

Today, we bridge the gap between technical execution and business reality. We will stop guessing at pricing and start building systems that scale. Using the precision of Antigravity and the rapid iteration of Claude Code, you will transform from a student coder into a SaaS architect capable of deploying production-grade pricing infrastructure.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Before touching code, define what you are selling. Is it usage-based, per-seat, or feature-gated? This prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my feature set and suggest a tiered pricing structure that balances user acquisition with long-term revenue growth. Identify the primary value metric.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Analyze my codebase and suggest a subscription schema based on my current feature set.'

steps.title: Architect the Billing Schema
steps.description: Design a database schema that supports multiple plans and allows for future migrations without breaking existing user data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS billing system including tables for Users, Subscriptions, Plans, and UsageLogs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a migration file for the billing schema in my database.'

steps.title: Integrate Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Connect your app to a payment gateway (like Stripe) using secure webhooks to handle subscription lifecycle events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a secure implementation pattern for handling Stripe webhooks to update user subscription status in a Node.js/Express environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Implement a webhook endpoint to handle checkout.session.completed events.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Protect your premium features with middleware that checks the user's active subscription status before executing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a reusable middleware function that checks if a user has access to a specific feature tier based on their database subscription record.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Apply the subscription-check middleware to all routes in the /premium directory.'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Finalize your deployment pipeline and set up logging to track conversion events and failed payments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a monitoring dashboard layout for tracking SaaS metrics like MRR, Churn, and ARPU based on my database schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a dashboard query to calculate Monthly Recurring Revenue from my current database structure.'

pro_tips: Always offer an annual discount; it boosts immediate cash flow and reduces churn.
Never hardcode your pricing tiers; store them in a configuration file or database to iterate without a redeploy.
Use 'Feature Flagging' to test pricing tiers on a small cohort of users before a full-scale launch.
Implement a 'Grace Period' for failed payments to prevent accidental churn of legitimate users.
Focus on 'Time-to-Value' as your primary KPI; if they can't see the value before the paywall, they won't pay.
content.next_steps: After deploying your billing system, focus on your go-to-market strategy. Start by building a landing page with a clear Call-to-Action to validate your pricing tiers with real users.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build a sustainable SaaS business. Stop waiting for the perfect moment and start coding the future of your portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
